siteDescription: Portfolio of Elsa van Dam — illustration, research, and workshops.

paragraphs: Elsa van Dam is an illustrator with a love for hand-drawn lines, soft colour palettes, and stories rooted in everyday life. She works across editorial, cultural, and social projects.

Her practice is informed by design research: listening carefully, making together, and translating complex ideas into accessible visual language. She believes illustration can open conversations and make space for new perspectives.
When she is not drawing, you will find her walking, reading, or collecting small observations that later find their way back into her sketchbooks.
For commissions, collaborations, or workshop requests, feel free to reach out.
an illustrator and visual researcher based in the Netherlands. My work moves between editorial illustration, participatory design, and narrative image-making.
With a background in design research, I create images that help people see complex stories more clearly — from zines and reports to workshops and visual tools.
This portfolio brings together selected work, ongoing research, and creative collaborations. Welcome.
Elsa facilitates illustration and visual thinking workshops for communities, students, and organisations. Sessions are hands-on, playful, and grounded in collective making.
Topics include storytelling through drawing, zine-making, visual research methods, and using illustration as a tool for dialogue. Workshops can be tailored to your group and context.
For enquiries about availability and custom programmes, please get in touch via the contact page.
